items.title: Emergency First Responders Say Waymos Are Getting Worse
items.summary: “I believe the technology was deployed too quickly in too vast amounts, with hundreds of vehicles, when it wasn’t really ready,” one police official told federal regulators last month.

items.title: Taylor Swift Wants to Trademark Her Likeness. These TikTok Deepfake Ads Show Why
items.summary: Researchers show scammers are using AI-manipulated footage of celebrity interviews to trick users into sharing their personal data.

items.title: Female Looksmaxxer Alorah Ziva Is Suing Clavicular for Alleged Battery
items.summary: Aleksandra Mendoza, aka Alorah Ziva, alleges that the 20-year-old influencer injected her with drugs on a livestream and had nonconsensual sex with her while she was underage.

items.title: Google Cloud surpasses $20B but says growth was capacity-constrained
items.summary: Google Cloud topped $20B in quarterly revenue for the first time, fueled by surging demand for AI. But capacity constraints mean it could have grown even faster.

items.title: Google gains 25M subscriptions in Q1, driven by YouTube and Google One
items.summary: Google added 25M paid subscriptions in Q1, reaching 350M total, as YouTube and Google One grow.

items.title: Fusion power startup Zap Energy pulls a partial pivot, adding nuclear fission to the mix
items.summary: Surprise! Fusion startup Zap Energy says it will be developing fission reactors alongside its fusion devices.

items.title: Oil price rises above $120 after reports of 'extended' Iran blockade
items.summary: The price of crude oil has swung sharply as uncertainty over the war in the Middle East continues.

items.title: Comey surrenders over charge of threatening Trump's life in Instagram post
items.summary: Prosecutors say a 2025 seashell photo posted by the former FBI director was a call for violence against Trump.

items.title: Hungary's next PM says frozen EU funds will be paid out soon
items.summary: Péter Magyar meets EU leaders in Brussels, for the first time since his Tisza party won a landslide election.

items.title: SoFi CEO defends decision to hold guidance steady
items.summary: SoFi CEO Anthony Noto explained that the company declined to raise its full-year outlook due to macro uncertainty, not weakness in fundamentals.

items.title: Jerome Powell says he will continue to serve as a Fed governor, calls Trump criticism 'unprecedented'
items.summary: Powell addressed the question of whether he will stay on as a Board of Governors member after his term as chair ends.

items.title: PayPal's new CEO makes Venmo a standalone business unit as potential buyers circle
items.summary: PayPal is betting that a new corporate structure can reignite growth at a company that has lost ground to Apple, Google and Stripe in the e-commerce battle.

items.title: Stocks Sink in Broad AI Rout Sparked by China's DeepSeek
items.summary: U.S. stocks were mostly lower, with the Nasdaq leading declines as makers of AI infrastructure suffered steep falls, many in the double digits. Nvidia was down 16%.

items.title: Comex Gold, Silver Settle Lower
items.summary: Gold settled 1.4% lower, down two of the past three sessions, and silver fell 2.5%, down three of the past four sessions.

items.title: DeepSeek Won't Sink U.S. AI Titans
items.summary: Panic fueling the selloff of Nvidia, Broadcom and other tech giants is overblown.

items.title: Chinese airbag parts tied to crash deaths banned
items.summary: The National Highway Traffic Safety Administration on Wednesday banned Chinese airbag inflators tied to at least 10 car crash deaths and injured two other people. NHTSA said an investigation revealed that frontal driver airbags exploded, sending large metal fragments into drivers’ chests, necks, eyes and faces. All recorded accidents took place in a GM or Hyundai…

items.title: 50-foot ancient snake discovered in India may be one of the largest ever
items.summary: A massive prehistoric snake discovered in India may rank among the largest ever to slither across Earth. Named Vasuki indicus, this ancient giant lived around 47 million years ago and is estimated to have stretched an astonishing 11 to 15 meters long—rivaling the legendary Titanoboa. Fossilized vertebrae unearthed from a lignite mine in Gujarat reveal a thick-bodied, powerful snake likely built for slow, stealthy ambush attacks, similar to modern anacondas.

items.title: Scientists catch antimatter “atom” acting like a wave for the first time
items.summary: Quantum physics once shocked scientists by revealing that particles can behave like waves—and now, that strange behavior has been pushed even further. For the first time, researchers have observed wave-like interference in positronium, an exotic “atom” made of an electron and its antimatter partner, a positron. This breakthrough not only strengthens the weird reality of quantum mechanics but also opens the door to new experiments involving antimatter, including the possibility of testing how gravity affects it—something never directly measured before.

items.title: A one-in-a-million supernova seen five times could reveal the Universe’s true speed
items.summary: A spectacular cosmic event nicknamed “SN Winny” could help solve one of astronomy’s biggest mysteries: how fast the universe is expanding. This rare superluminous supernova, located 10 billion light-years away, appears five times in the sky thanks to gravitational lensing, creating a dazzling “cosmic fireworks” effect. By measuring the slight delays between each appearance—caused by light taking different paths around two foreground galaxies—scientists can directly calculate the universe’s expansion rate.

items.title: Prenatal surgery for spina bifida may get a boost from stem cells
items.summary: A clinical trial for spina bifida treatment suggests that a surgical approach relying on stem cells is safe for patients. Its efficacy is still being evaluated.

items.title: Seismic data captured the sound of awe during a solar eclipse
items.summary: From the hush of people coming to a standstill to the reverberations of fans, seismic data can capture the ebbs and flows of human activity.

items.title: Can ‘extinct’ volcanoes still erupt? A Greek peak holds surprising clues
items.summary: Tiny crystals suggest extinct volcanoes could still grow underground, a finding that could reshape how scientists assess eruption risk.
